name: Camera FOV health check
description: Daily sanity check that a camera is actually doing its job. Pulls a still from one camera, asks Gemini whether the view is impaired (obstructed, blurry, mis-aimed at a wall, dirty lens) and posts a Helix event whenever something looks wrong so maintenance can investigate. Pick the camera and adjust the schedule before enabling. Clone the flow per camera for coverage of more than one.
summary: Daily schedule → Gemini still-image (JSON) → condition → Helix event when impaired

config.prompt:
You are auditing a security camera frame for image-quality / mounting issues. Decide whether this camera appears to be doing its job, OR whether the lens / mounting is impaired in a way a maintenance technician should investigate.

Respond with ONLY a JSON object - no prose, no code fence - with exactly four keys:
  "status":   "ok" if the image looks like a normal working security camera view; "issue" if the camera itself appears impaired.
  "issue":    when status is "issue", one of: "obstructed" (lens covered), "blurry" (severely out of focus), "mis_aimed" (pointed at a wall / ceiling / floor as if knocked), "dirty_lens" (smears, water, fog), "dark" (uniformly black/white in a way IR / exposure can't explain), "scene_drift" (view doesn't look like a security cam should see). Use null when status is "ok".
  "severity": "low" / "medium" / "high". Use null when status is "ok".
  "reasoning": one-sentence explanation, max 180 characters.

DO NOT flag legitimate situations:
  - A dark scene at night is fine (working IR cameras are dark).
  - An empty hallway, parking lot, loading dock, or wall corner is fine if it appears to be the intended view.
  - Slight motion blur, compression artifacts, or wide-angle distortion in an otherwise normal frame.
  - Privacy-masked regions (solid gray / black rectangles in part of the frame) - those are intentional.
  - Glare, sun flare, or backlit scenes if the rest is intelligible.

DO flag:
  - Lens physically covered, sprayed, or fogged so the scene is unintelligible.
  - Camera so out of focus the scene can't be identified.
  - Camera knocked out of alignment and now pointing at a wall, ceiling, or floor.
  - Entire frame is a single flat color suggesting covered lens or broken sensor.

Examples:
  {"status": "issue", "issue": "obstructed", "severity": "high", "reasoning": "Lens covered by fabric or paper - no scene visible."}
  {"status": "issue", "issue": "mis_aimed", "severity": "medium", "reasoning": "Camera appears to be pointing at a ceiling tile rather than the corridor."}
  {"status": "ok", "issue": null, "severity": null, "reasoning": "Normal parking lot view, daytime, clear focus."}
